author: @aeime6747

text:
This movie blew my mind. I expected a lesser outing than its predecessor and I am so glad that I was wrong. There is so much meaning in this movie about right vs wrong, morals, judgement vs acceptance, leadership, hypocrisy, oppression, human rights, rights of all living creatures, evolution, courage vs fear.... those are just the ones I connected with, maybe you'll make your own meanings. The dialogue is clever and very funny at times without trying too hard. Overall its a great movie that gets the following points across in an entertaining, non-preaching way: 
1. That there is good and bad in all of us and that we shouldn't judge others because those doing the judging are usually the ones who won't bring anything to the table when we need to pull together in a crisis. 
2. The ones being judged are the ones who try harder in a crisis because they either: 
- feel that they have nothing to lose, or 
- want to prove their critics wrong, or
- desperately want to be accepted, or
- simply want to feel better about themselves.

For me it's a 10/10

text: NO SPOILERS! I just finished watching the movie and I don't want to talk too much about the movie, but it was actually better than I expected it would've been. It is as if DC has turned the page to the next chapter in the history of DC movies. Thought the new faces in the squad would make us forget about the old ones. but do not fear. The movie truly makes up for it. This movie undoubtedly has proven to us that the DC universe doesn't always have to follow the dark theme. If DC keeps up the good work and actually delivers us some actual decent DC movies like it used to. The DC universe won't just survive, but it will thrive. I do recommend watching the movie.
